options.description: This is the title that will be displayed in the header for this column
This is the key for this column in the data array
Determines if the column is visible. (see Column Visibility for more details)
Sets the horizontal text alignment for this column (left|center|right)

enum.description: Align cell content to the left
Align cell content to the center
Align cell content to the right

options.description: Sets the vertical text alignment for this column (top|middle|bottom)

enum.description: Align cell content to the top
Align cell content to the middle
Align cell content to the bottom

options.description: Sets the horizontal text alignment for this columns header title (left|center|right)

enum.description: Align header title to the left
Align header title to the center
Align header title to the right

options.description: Sets the width of this column, this can be set in pixels or as a percentage of total table width (if not set the system will determine the best)
Sets the minimum width of this column, this should be set in pixels
Sets the maximum width of this column, this should be set in pixels
Sets the maximum width of this column when it is first rendered, the user can then resize to above this (up to the maxWidth, if set) this should be set in pixels
When using fitColumns layout mode, determines how much the column should grow to fill available space (see Table Layout for more details)
When using fitColumns layout mode, determines how much the column should shrink to fit available space (see Table Layout for more details)
Set whether column can be resized by user dragging its edges (see Table Layout for more details)

enum.description: Column can be resized by dragging either the header or cell edges
Column cannot be resized
Column can only be resized by dragging the header edge
Column can only be resized by dragging the cell edges

options.description: Freezes the column in place when scrolling (see Frozen Columns for more details)
An integer to determine when the column should be hidden in responsive mode (see Responsive Layout for more details)
Sets the on hover tooltip for each cell in this column (see Formatting Data for more details)
Sets css classes on header and cells in this column. (value should be a string containing space separated class names)
Sets the column as a row handle, allowing it to be used to drag movable rows. (see Movable Rows for more details)
Show or hide column in the getHtml function output (see Retrieve Data as HTML Table for more details)
Show or hide column in the print output (see Printing for more details)
Show or hide column in downloaded data (see Downloading Table Data for more details)
Set custom title for column in download (see Downloading Table Data for more details)
Show or hide column in the clipboard output (see Clipboard for more details)
Alter the row height to fit the contents of the cell instead of hiding overflow
Determines how to sort data in this column (see Sorting Data for more details)

enum.description: Sorts column as a string of characters

locale.description: A BCP 47 language tag string for locale-aware sorting, or true to use the browser default locale

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom of the column

enum.description: Sorts column as a number (integer or float)

decimal.description: Character used to represent the decimal point (default '.')

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

enum.description: Sorts column as alphanumeric data (handles numbers within strings correctly)

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

enum.description: Sorts column as a boolean value

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

enum.description: Sorts column by whether a value exists (non-empty) or not

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

enum.description: Sorts column as a date value

format.description: The date format string to use for parsing (default 'DD/MM/YYYY')

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

enum.description: Sorts column as a time value

format.description: The time format string to use for parsing (default 'hh:mm')

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

enum.description: Sorts column as a datetime value

format.description: The datetime format string to use for parsing

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

enum.description: Sorts column by a value derived from a cell's array value

type.description: How to derive the sort value from the array

alignEmptyValues.description: Whether to sort empty values to the top or bottom

options.description: Additional parameters you can pass to the sorter(see Sorting Data for more details)
Set how you would like the data to be formatted (see Formatting Data for more details)

enum.description: Renders cell value as plain text, safe for user data
Renders cell value as multi-line text, adjusting row height to fit
Renders cell value as raw HTML â€” use only with trusted data
Formats a number as a monetary value with currency symbol, thousand separators and decimal places

decimal.description: Symbol to represent the decimal point (default '.')

thousand.description: Symbol to represent the thousand separator (default ',')

symbol.description: Currency symbol to place before or after the number

symbolAfter.description: Place the currency symbol after the number instead of before

negativeSign.description: Use a minus sign instead of parentheses for negative values

precision.description: Number of decimal places (default 2, or false to use value as-is)

enum.description: Renders the cell value as an image element

height.description: CSS height for the image element

width.description: CSS width for the image element

urlPrefix.description: String to prepend to the cell value to form the image URL

urlSuffix.description: String to append to the cell value to form the image URL

elementAttributes.description: Object of attributes to set on the img element

enum.description: Renders the cell value as a clickable anchor tag

labelField.description: Row data field to use as the link label

label.description: Fixed string or function returning the link label

urlPrefix.description: String to prepend to the cell value to form the URL

urlField.description: Row data field to use as the URL

url.description: Fixed string or function returning the URL

target.description: Value for the link's target attribute (e.g. '_blank')

download.description: Set the download attribute; true uses the filename from the URL, or provide a string filename

enum.description: Transforms a date/time value from one format to another (requires luxon.js)

inputFormat.description: The luxon format string for parsing the input value (default 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'), or 'iso' for ISO 8601 strings

outputFormat.description: The luxon format string for the displayed output (default 'dd/MM/yyyy HH:mm:ss')

invalidPlaceholder.description: Value to display when the cell value cannot be parsed; true shows the original value

timezone.description: IANA timezone string to use when parsing/displaying the date

enum.description: Shows the difference between a cell date value and now (or a reference date) (requires luxon.js)

inputFormat.description: The luxon format string for parsing the cell value (default 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss'), or 'iso' for ISO 8601 strings

humanize.description: Display the difference in a humanized form (e.g. '2 days ago')

unit.description: Time unit for the difference; when humanize is true an array of units can be passed (default 'seconds')

suffix.description: Append an 'ago' or 'from now' suffix to the output

date.description: Reference date to compare against (luxon DateTime object, defaults to now)

invalidPlaceholder.description: Placeholder to display when the cell value cannot be parsed

timezone.description: IANA timezone string to use when parsing the date

enum.description: Displays a green tick if the value is truthy, and a red cross if not

allowEmpty.description: Show an empty cell instead of a cross for empty values (undefined, null, '') (default false)

allowTruthy.description: Show a tick for any truthy value (default false)

tickElement.description: Custom HTML for the tick element; set to false to hide ticks

crossElement.description: Custom HTML for the cross element; set to false to hide crosses

enum.description: Looks up the cell value in a supplied object and displays the matching value

(key).description: Pass an object where keys are cell data values and values are the display strings

enum.description: Joins array values into a string separated by a delimiter (default ',')

delimiter.description: Character used to join the array values (default '|')

valueMap.description: Property path (dot notation) or map function to extract a value from each array item

enum.description: Pretty-prints objects and arrays using JSON.stringify

multiline.description: Pretty print output across multiple lines (default true)

indent.description: Indent character used for object properties (default '\t')

replacer.description: The replacer argument for JSON.stringify (see MDN docs)

enum.description: Renders the cell value as a star rating

stars.description: Maximum number of stars to display (default 5)

enum.description: Renders the cell value as a traffic-light coloured circle

min.description: Minimum value for the colour scale (default 0)

max.description: Maximum value for the colour scale (default 100)

color.description: CSS colour string, array of colours, or function returning a colour

enum.description: Renders the cell value as a progress bar

min.description: Minimum value for the progress bar (default 0)

max.description: Maximum value for the progress bar (default 100)

color.description: CSS colour, array of colours, or function returning a colour for the bar fill

legend.description: Text label on the bar; true shows the percentage, or a function can return a string

legendColor.description: CSS colour for the legend text

legendAlign.description: Horizontal alignment of the legend text

enum.description: Sets the cell's background colour to the cell value (expected to be a valid CSS colour string)
Renders a green tick button element in the cell
Renders a red cross button element in the cell
Renders the row number (position in the current dataset) in the cell
Renders a row move handle for use with movableRows
Renders a checkbox for selecting the row
Renders the expand/collapse toggle for responsive layout hidden columns
Renders the cell value as a toggleable on/off switch

size.description: Size of the switch in pixels (default 15)

onValue.description: Cell value that represents the on state (default true)

offValue.description: Cell value that represents the off state (default false)

onTruthy.description: Treat any truthy value as the on state (default false)

onColor.description: CSS colour of the switch when in the on state

offColor.description: CSS colour of the switch when in the off state

clickable.description: Allow the switch to be clicked to toggle the cell value

enum.description: Automatically picks a formatter based on the cell value type; can be customised with formatterLookup and paramsLookup

formatterLookup.description: Function passed the CellComponent; must return the formatter to use

paramsLookup.description: Object keyed by formatter name with params values, or a function returning the params for the chosen formatter

options.description: Additional parameters you can pass to the formatter(see Formatting Data for more details)
Set how you would like the data to be formatted when the table is printed(see Formatting Data for more details)
Additional parameters you can pass to the print formatter(see Formatting Data for more details)
Set how you would like the data to be formatted when copied to the clipboard(see Formatting Data for more details)
Additional parameters you can pass to the clipboard formatter(see Formatting Data for more details)
Set how you would like the data to be formatted when the getHtml function is used(see Formatting Data for more details)
Additional parameters you can pass to the html output formatter(see Formatting Data for more details)
Callback to check if the cell is editable (see Manipulating Data for more details)
Set the editor to be used when editing the data. (see Manipulating Data for more details)

enum.description: Single-line text input editor

search.description: Use a search-type input element instead of text

mask.description: Input mask string â€” A=letter, 9=number, *=any character

maskAutoFill.description: Auto-fill mask literal characters as the user types

maskLetterChar.description: Character used to represent a letter in the mask (default 'A')

maskNumberChar.description: Character used to represent a number in the mask (default '9')

maskWildcardChar.description: Character used to represent a wildcard in the mask (default '*')

selectContents.description: Select the contents of the cell when the editor opens

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set directly on the input element (e.g. { maxlength: '10' })

enum.description: Multi-line textarea editor that expands vertically to fit content

maskAutoFill.description: Auto-fill mask literal characters as the user types

selectContents.description: Select the contents of the cell when the editor opens

verticalNavigation.description: How the up/down arrow keys navigate

shiftEnterSubmit.description: Submit the edit when Shift+Enter is pressed

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the textarea element

enum.description: Numeric input editor for integer or float values

maskAutoFill.description: Auto-fill mask literal characters as the user types

selectContents.description: Select the contents of the cell when the editor opens

verticalNavigation.description: How the up/down arrow keys navigate

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the input element

enum.description: Range slider editor

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the range input element

enum.description: Boolean tick/cross toggle editor

tristate.description: Allow a third indeterminate null state

indeterminateValue.description: The data value assigned to the indeterminate state (default null)

trueValue.description: The data value assigned to the ticked state (default true)

falseValue.description: The data value assigned to the crossed state (default false)

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the checkbox element

enum.description: Star rating editor

stars.description: Maximum number of stars to display (default 5)

enum.description: Dropdown list editor with optional autocomplete and multi-select

values.description: List of values to display. Array of strings, array of {label, value} objects, or a function returning either

valuesURL.description: URL to fetch list values from

valuesLookup.description: Look up values from existing column data

valuesLookupField.description: Column field to use when performing a valuesLookup

clearable.description: Allow the selected value to be cleared

itemFormatter.description: Function to format individual list items

sort.description: Sort order for the list items

emptyValue.description: The data value to use for an empty selection

multiselect.description: Allow multiple items to be selected; set to an integer to limit the count

autocomplete.description: Enable autocomplete filtering of the list as the user types

filterFunc.description: Custom function to filter the list items

filterDelay.description: Milliseconds to wait after a keystroke before filtering the list

allowEmpty.description: Allow an empty string to be a valid selection

listOnEmpty.description: Show the full list when the autocomplete input is empty

freetext.description: Allow the user to type a value not present in the list

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the input element

verticalNavigation.description: How the up/down arrow keys navigate when the list is closed

enum.description: Native HTML date picker editor

min.description: Minimum selectable date string in the format matching 'format'

format.description: The luxon format string for parsing/formatting the date value (default 'yyyy-MM-dd')

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the date input element

verticalNavigation.description: How the up/down arrow keys navigate

enum.description: Native HTML time picker editor

format.description: The luxon format string for the time value (default 'HH:mm')

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the time input element

verticalNavigation.description: How the up/down arrow keys navigate

enum.description: Native HTML datetime-local picker editor

format.description: The luxon format string for the datetime value (default 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss')

elementAttributes.description: Object of HTML attributes to set on the datetime input element

verticalNavigation.description: How the up/down arrow keys navigate

options.description: Additional parameters you can pass to the editor (see Manipulating Data for more details)

enum.description: Value must not be empty
Value must be unique across all rows in the column
Value must be a valid integer
Value must be a valid float
Value must be numeric (integer or float)
Value must be a string
Value must contain only alphanumeric characters
Value must be a valid email address format
Value must be a valid URL format
Value must be a valid date (use sorterParams format if needed)
Value must be a valid time
Value must be a valid datetime
Value must be greater than or equal to the parameter (e.g. 'min:5')
Value must be less than or equal to the parameter (e.g. 'max:100')
String value must be at least N characters long (e.g. 'minLength:3')
String value must be no more than N characters long (e.g. 'maxLength:20')
Value must be one of the pipe-separated values (e.g. 'in:yes|no|maybe')
